‘They were better than us,’ said Lampard. ‘They were stronger physically, in the mind, with the ball and when that’s the case, you lose the game.

‘They were stronger than us. We don’t have crowds at the minute, so you feel and hear every voice, and all I could hear was Sheffield United voices, so they were stronger in that sense. They’re a good team, with good players. We all know that, so if you come here and perform below-par collectively, as we did, it’s going to happen.’

‘We matched them because we have to respect their shape,’ added Frank. ‘We were too slow with the ball, we weren’t even getting our wingers into the game and they have been fantastic since the restart. We tried to get some more control of the game, which we did for a spell, but we never looked overly threatening and they still had the counter-attack.

‘I’ve got to be careful not to over-analyse this,’ said Frank. ‘We have to move forward and what we have to do now is put today’s game to bed.

‘The only thing that matters now is that we fight for the top four and we’re still in that race because of how we’ve been this season. We have to put this game away very quickly but I learnt a lot, I won’t forget that.

‘I’m not concerned so much about top four today, and all I do is watch the games on TV and consider where we’re at and what we want to do. Of course I want to get there but today I’m concerned about how we performed and I learned a lot about that. We cannot approach Norwich or Man United or Liverpool or Wolves in the same way we did today.'
